name: Staffing Ratios
consequences: Adjusting staffing ratios could ensure safe operations, but may compromise data quality or increase costs.
options: Maintain current ratios for efficiency
Increase technician-to-participant ratio to enhance safety
Implement a hybrid model with rotating night shifts and on-site specialists
review: Staffing Ratios Review: The options neglect the impact of staff fatigue and turnover, which could compromise data quality and participant safety.

name: Participant Compensation
consequences: Increasing compensation could improve recruitment, but may inflate costs or create undue incentives.
options: Maintain current per-night compensation of €80
Increase to €120 per night for improved retention
Offer a one-time bonus for participants with captured events
review: Participant Compensation Review: The options overlook the potential impact on participant motivation and the trade-off between cost savings and data quality.

name: Data Sharing
consequences: Expanding data sharing could accelerate scientific progress, but may compromise participant privacy.
options: Limit public data sharing to de-identified physiological and sensor data
Share bedroom video with select researchers under strict confidentiality agreements
Deposite raw video in a secure repository for future analysis
review: Data Sharing Review: The options miss the tension between scientific collaboration and participant privacy, which could compromise trust and data quality.

name: Pharmaceutical Partnership
consequences: Securing a pharmaceutical partnership could enhance funding, but may compromise academic freedom.
options: Maintain current independent research focus
Partner with a pharma company for co-funding and expertise
Explore alternative partnerships with industry or government
review: Pharmaceutical Partnership Review: The options neglect the potential impact on publication rights, data ownership, and the scientific program's integrity.

name: Expansion and Intervention Studies
consequences: Pursuing phase-two grant funding could accelerate research progress, but may compromise the current study's focus.
options: Prioritize completion of Aim 1-3 goals
Develop a phased expansion plan with clear milestones and timelines
Submit a phase-two grant proposal for concurrent investigation
review: Expansion and Intervention Studies Review: The options overlook the potential trade-off between expanding the research scope and compromising the current study's integrity.

name: Governance Structure
consequences: Establishing a more robust governance structure could ensure accountability, but may compromise operational efficiency.
options: Maintain current external scientific advisory board
Implement a hybrid model with rotating committee members and on-site specialists
Establish an internal research management office
review: Governance Structure Review: The options neglect the potential impact of governance changes on operational flexibility, participant safety, and data quality.

name: Participant Recruitment
consequences: Adjusting recruitment strategies could improve enrollment rates, but may compromise participant diversity or increase costs.
options: Maintain current channels (University Hospital Bonn sleep clinic, regional neurologist referrals)
Expand to online advertising and social media outreach
Target specific patient populations (e.g., those with comorbid conditions)
review: Participant Recruitment Review: The options overlook the potential impact of recruitment changes on participant demographics, data quality, and research validity.

name: Technician Training
consequences: Upgrading research technicians' skills for data annotation and event scoring will improve data quality, but may require additional training time and resources.
options: Develop a comprehensive onboarding program with hands-on experience in data annotation and event scoring
Collaborate with sleep medicine experts to create specialized training modules for technicians
Implement a peer-to-peer mentoring system where experienced technicians train new staff members
review: The options neglect the potential impact of technician turnover on data quality and consistency, introducing variability that may compromise the study's validity.

name: Participant Engagement
consequences: Enhancing participant engagement through personalized support and feedback will increase data yield, but may require additional resources for staff and infrastructure.
options: Develop a participant-centered approach with regular check-ins and progress updates
Implement a rewards system to incentivize participants to share their experiences and provide detailed accounts of their parasomnia events
Create a participant advisory board to gather feedback and suggestions on improving the research experience
review: The options assume that all participants will be highly motivated and engaged, ignoring potential issues with retention or drop-out rates.

name: Sensor Calibration
consequences: Ensuring accurate sensor calibration will improve data quality, but may require additional resources for equipment maintenance and technician expertise.
options: Implement a rigorous calibration protocol to ensure accuracy across all sensors
Collaborate with industry experts to develop custom calibration solutions for the residential monitoring system
Develop an in-house calibration team to maintain and update sensor configurations
review: The options neglect the potential impact of equipment failure or sensor drift on data quality, compromising the study's validity.

name: Recruitment Strategy
consequences: Developing an effective recruitment strategy will increase participant numbers, but may require additional resources for outreach and advertising.
options: Implement a multi-channel approach with targeted social media campaigns and community outreach
Collaborate with regional neurologists to refer patients and promote the study
Develop a referral program to incentivize healthcare professionals to participate
review: The options neglect the potential impact of participant demographics on data quality, compromising the study's validity.

name: Participant Retention
consequences: Improving participant retention through enhanced support and engagement will increase data yield, but may require additional resources for staff and infrastructure.
options: Develop a participant-centered approach with regular check-ins and progress updates
Implement a rewards system to incentivize participants to share their experiences and provide detailed accounts of their parasomnia events
Create a participant advisory board to gather feedback and suggestions on improving the research experience
review: The options assume that all participants will be highly motivated and engaged, ignoring potential issues with retention or drop-out rates.
